name: Salmon Timbales with Mornay Sauce
recipeIngredient: 3/2 cup Ripe Or Stuffed Olives, cut in wedges
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut olives into wedges and set aside.
2. Drain salmon and save the liquid. Remove and discard skin and large bones from salmon. Flake fish.
3. Melt butter and stir in flour, salt, pepper, and tarragon. Gradually add milk to the liquid from drained salmon until it makes 1 1/3 cups. Add tomato paste.
4. Cook the mixture by stirring constantly until it boils and thickens. Stir in tomato paste.
5. Mix together salmon, olives, and bread crumbs. Beat eggs until bubbly and stir into the salmon mixture. Add the thickened, seasoned sauce and mix well.
6. Spoon the mixture into five (6 oz.) custard cups that have been well greased. Place the cups in a pan holding about 1 inch of hot water.
7. Bake at 350°F for about 50 minutes or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean. Remove from water and let stand 5 minutes.
8. Unmold and serve with Mornay Sauce.
FOR THE MORNAY SAUCE:
1. Melt butter.
2. Add flour. Gradually add milk, stirring constantly, until it boils and thickens.
3. Add salt, pepper, and cheese. Continue to stir until the cheese melts.
Makes 1¾ cups sauce.
